name: Google Weather Lookup
description: Look up current weather for a location via Google Search's weather widget.

browser_automation.description: Hits google.com/search?q=weather+LOCATION and reads the inline weather widget (#wob_wc).

sequences.name: google_weather_extract
sequences.description: Extract current conditions from the Google Search weather widget.

required_variables.name: location
required_variables.description: City or location name (e.g. 'San Francisco', 'Paris, France', 'Tokyo').

steps.name: Navigate to Google Search weather query
Extract widget fields (location label sits in a sibling section, others are inside #wob_wc)

help.summary: Get current weather conditions for a location from Google Search's weather widget.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: City or location name. Use 'City, Country' for disambiguation if needed.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google weather --param location="San Francisco, CA"
rzn-browser run google weather --param location="Paris, France"
examples.description: Paris weather, disambiguated from Paris, TX.

help.returns: Object: { location, time, condition, temperature, precipitation, humidity, wind }. Temperature unit (F vs C) follows the user's regional setting on Google.
notes: The widget toggles F/C based on Google's locale guess for the session — the workflow does not force a unit. Inspect the temperature field's trailing unit if you need to normalize.
Forecast (next-day) extraction is not included; this is current conditions only.
